title: Blowup analysis for a quasi-exact 1D model of 3D Euler and Navier-Stokes

thm:1.2.summary: For the inviscid weak advection model with a $\in (1-\delta, 1)$, there exists $C^\infty$ initial data producing a self-similar finite-time singularity that is neither expanding nor focusing; blowup has the form $\omega(x,t) = (1 + c_{u,\infty}t)^{-1} \omega_\infty$ with blowup time T $= -1/c_{u,\infty}$.

rem:1.1.summary: Relates the weak advection model to the generalized Constantin-Lax-Majda (gCLM) model; De Gregorio (a=1) is the special case; both share competition between advection and vortex stretching.

rem:1.3.summary: Numerical evidence for neither-expanding-nor-focusing self-similar blowup at a $\in [0.6, 0.9]$; analogous to gCLM model behavior; $c_{u,\infty} = 2(a-1) +$ o(a-1) implies very large blowup time for a near 1.

thm:1.4.summary: For the original inviscid Hou-Li model with $\alpha \in (1-\delta$₀, 1), there exists $C^\alpha$ initial data producing a self-similar blowup of the same neither-expanding-nor-focusing type as Theorem 1.2.

rem:1.5.summary: Sharpness of $C^\alpha$ blowup: blowup time T $= O(-1/(2(\alpha-1)))$; no blowup for intrinsically low-regularity $C^\varepsilon$ data $(\varepsilon$ near 0), which is conjectured to develop focusing singularity instead.

rem:1.6.summary: Well-posedness for $C^1$ data (Hou-Li 2008) is sharp: any weakening of advection or slight reduction in regularity induces self-similar blowup.

thm:1.7.summary: For the viscous weak advection model with a $\in (1-\delta$₁, 1), there exists $C^\infty$ initial data that develops a finite-time singularity.

rem:2.1.summary: No spatial rescaling is introduced since the blowup is neither focusing nor expanding; the scaling factors for u, $\omega, \psi$ are identical.

lem:2.2.summary: Integration by parts identity: (sin x $\cdot f_x$, f $\rho) =$ ½$(f^2, \rho)$; directly verified, used to compute energy contributions from the advection term.

lem:2.3.summary: Complete orthonormal basis for $L^2(\rho)$: odd functions have basis $o^k =$ sin(kx) - sin((k-1)x); even functions have basis $e^k =$ cos(kx) - cos((k+1)x); used for exact Fourier mode computations.

prop:2.4.summary: Energy estimate for the leading linearized operators: dE₁ = ((L₁)_x, $u_x \rho) + (L$₂, $\omega \rho) \le -0.16[(u_x, u_x \rho) + (\omega, \omega \rho)]$; establishes linear damping at rate 0.16.

lem:3.1.summary: Nonlinear norm estimates: weighted $L^2$ control ‖$\psi$‖$_\rho$, ‖$\psi_x - \psi_x(0)$‖$_\rho$, ‖u‖$_\rho$ ≲ E; and $L^\infty$ control ‖$\psi_x$‖$_\infty$, ‖$\psi/sin$ x‖$_\infty$, ‖u‖$_\infty$ ≲ E; enables closure of the nonlinear energy estimate.

rem:3.2.summary: Weighted norm design: leading local terms of linearized operators are $-2D_x u_x$ and $-2D_x \omega$; in the new norm, combined terms give damping $(-2D_x D_x u_x, D_x u_x \rho) + (-2D_x D_x \omega, D_x \omega \rho) = -K^2$.

lem:4.1.summary: Explicit integral formula for the stream function: for odd periodic $\omega$ with $-\psi_xx = \omega$, one has $\psi_x(0) = -1/(2\pi) \int$ y $\omega(y)$ dy and $\psi = \int$₀ˣ $(y-x) \omega(y)$ dy + x $\psi_x(0)$.

lem:4.2.summary: For Hölder profiles with $\kappa = 7/8 < 9/10 < \alpha < 1$: pointwise residue bounds $|\partial$ᵢ_x $\omega_res| + |\partial$ᵢ_x $u_res|$ ≲ $|\alpha-1| |sinx|^{\kappa-i}$ and refined cancellation estimates for the leading-order Hölder terms.

rem:5.1.summary: Viscous rescaling introduces extra degree of freedom $C_u(0)$, chosen to ensure the viscous term has smaller scaling than the main blowup terms.

rem:5.2.summary: Viscous energy norm combines a singularly-weighted energy norm and high-order Sobolev norms; criterion 1 (damping) verified in linear estimates; damping in (k-1)-th order norms dominates positive terms in k-th order norms.

lem:6.1.summary: Positivity of the Fourier quadratic form $F(a,c) \ge 0$ for all finite-energy sequences; the explicit sum formula involves coupling between odd and even Fourier modes and verifies the 0.16 damping coefficient in prop:2.4.

rem:6.2.summary: Three-step interval arithmetic verification that the smallest eigenvalue of the truncated $200\times200$ Fourier matrix F^(200) exceeds 0.01: approximate SVD via Matlab, residual bound via 1-norm $\le 200\times10^{-10}$, positive definiteness concluded.

edges.note: Integration by parts identity is used to compute (sin x $f_x$, f $\rho)$ terms in the energy estimate for the linearized advection operator
Fourier basis used for exact low-frequency mode computation in the energy estimate; exact orthonormal structure enables the 0.16 damping coefficient
Positivity of $F(a,c) \ge 0$ is the rigorous verification that the Fourier-mode energy estimate yields genuine damping; without this lem:6.1, prop:2.4 would not hold
F(a,c) is expressed in terms of the odd/even Fourier basis $o^k$ and $e^k$ from lem:2.3
Weighted $L^2$ estimates for $\psi$ use the integration by parts identity to control $\int$ sin x $\cdot \psi_x \cdot \psi \rho$ terms
Prop:2.4 is the central linear damping estimate; thm:1.2 closes the nonlinear argument by showing the nonlinear terms are controlled by the linear damping
Norm estimates from lem:3.1 bound the nonlinear terms in the energy estimate, enabling closure of the argument
Same self-similar blowup mechanism extended from smooth $(C^\infty)$ to Hölder $(C^\alpha)$ initial data for the original Hou-Li model; same neither-expanding-nor-focusing structure
Explicit integral formula for $\psi$ is needed to analyze the Hölder profile at the scale of |sin $x|^{\kappa}$
Residue estimates and refined cancellation bounds are the key technical input for controlling the Hölder profile perturbation
Energy estimate structure is reused for the viscous case; damping mechanism is the same but combined with Sobolev norms to handle viscous instability
Self-similar blowup framework from the inviscid case carries over; viscous terms are shown to be subleading via the combined norm in rem:5.2

self_similar_ansatz.description: Setup of the blowup structure: self-similar profile that is neither expanding nor focusing, uniform scaling of $u/\omega/\psi$

fourier_basis_and_identities.description: Analytic tools for the linearized energy estimate: integration by parts identity and orthonormal Fourier basis for odd/even periodic functions

linear_damping_estimate.description: Core energy estimate for the linearized system: exact Fourier computation yields 0.16 damping coefficient for the leading operators

nonlinear_norm_closure.description: Weighted $L^2$ and $L^\infty$ estimates enabling nonlinear closure; weighted norm designed to propagate damping through Dₓ

holder_extension.description: Additional tools for extending smooth blowup to Hölder $C^\alpha$ initial data: explicit stream function formula and residue estimates

viscous_extension.description: Norm design for the viscous case: extra degree of freedom $C_u(0)$ and combined singularly-weighted plus Sobolev norm
